people.text: Mia wants to join a club where she can build small robots. She is new to coding and needs beginner support.
Josh enjoys acting but feels nervous on stage. He wants a friendly group that practises short scenes.
Suki likes writing and wants feedback on stories. She prefers a quiet club after school.
Adam wants to play team sports, but he cannot stay late because he catches the school bus.
Lena cares about the environment and wants to help with practical projects around the school.

texts.text: Story Circle meets in the library after school. Members write short fiction and receive [8]careful feedback[/8] in a quiet, supportive group.
Eco Team organises recycling checks, garden planting and energy-saving posters. Members complete [10]practical environmental projects[/10] around the school.
Robot Makers introduces students to simple machines and basic coding. Beginners receive step-by-step help while they [6]build small robots[/6] in pairs.
Advanced Orchestra is for students who already play an instrument at a high level.
Chess Challenge prepares experienced players for weekend competitions against other schools.
Lunch League offers [9]football and basketball games during the lunch break[/9]. Students can enjoy team sports [9]without staying after school[/9], so it suits those catching buses.
Drama Starters uses games and [7]short scenes[/7] to help students become more confident. The teacher creates a friendly atmosphere for pupils who feel nervous performing.
Exam Club gives extra mathematics practice before final tests.

answers.explanation: Mia wants beginner robot-building with coding support.
Josh needs friendly drama practice for confidence.
Suki wants quiet story writing with feedback.
Adam needs team sports without staying late.
